title: UVStyle-Net: Unsupervised Few-shot Learning of 3D Style Similarity Measure for B-Reps
abstract: UVStyle-Net proposes a style similarity measure for B-Reps that leverages second-order statistics of activations in a pre-trained unsupervised 3D encoder. It learns to capture subjective end-user preferences with limited or unlabeled data using few-shot learning.
method_summary: Builds on UV-Net encoder, extracts Gram matrices from intermediate activations as style descriptors. Uses few-shot metric learning to adapt to user-specific style preferences without large labeled datasets.

key_results.style_retrieval: captures subjective style preferences with few examples

title: GC-CAD: Self-supervised Graph Neural Network for Mechanical CAD Retrieval
abstract: GC-CAD is a self-supervised contrastive graph neural network for mechanical CAD retrieval that directly models parameterized CAD raw files. Combines structure-aware representation learning with contrastive graph learning, achieving up to 100x efficiency improvement over baselines.
method_summary: Converts CAD files to graphs preserving parametric structure. Uses contrastive learning with graph augmentations for self-supervised pre-training. Two modules: structure-aware GNN encoder and contrastive learning framework.

title: BRepFormer: Transformer-Based B-rep Geometric Feature Recognition
abstract: BRepFormer is a transformer-based model for identifying both machining and complex CAD model features. It encodes and fuses geometric and topological features with bias combining edge and topology features to reinforce geometric constraints. Introduces Complex B-rep Feature Dataset (CBF) with 20,000 models.
method_summary: Transformer architecture that processes B-rep faces as tokens with topology-aware attention bias. Fuses edge features and topology features during each transformer iteration to reinforce geometric constraints on face representations.

title: OSCAR: Open-Set CAD Retrieval from a Language Prompt and a Single Image
abstract: OSCAR is a training-free method for retrieving matching 3D object models from unlabeled databases using language prompts and single images. Two-stage retrieval: text-based filtering using CLIP followed by image-based refinement using DINOv2.
method_summary: Generates multi-view renderings with captions for database models. At query time, uses CLIP for text-based candidate filtering, then DINOv2 for visual refinement. Training-free approach leveraging foundation models.

key_results.open_set_retrieval: strong performance on MI3DOR and YCB-V

title: DiffCAD: Weakly-Supervised Probabilistic CAD Model Retrieval and Alignment from an RGB Image
abstract: DiffCAD is the first weakly-supervised probabilistic approach to CAD retrieval and alignment from an RGB image. Uses diffusion models to learn likely distributions of CAD model retrieval and 9-DoF pose alignment, trained on synthetic data.
method_summary: Formulates CAD retrieval and alignment as conditional generation using diffusion models. Learns probabilistic distributions over shape retrieval and 9-DoF pose from synthetic training data, generalizing to real images.

title: CADSketchNet: An Annotated Sketch Dataset for 3D CAD Model Retrieval with Deep Neural Networks
abstract: CADSketchNet is a dataset for building sketch-based 3D CAD model retrieval systems using deep learning. Contains annotated sketches paired with 3D CAD models from available databases for training retrieval networks.
method_summary: Creates annotated sketch-CAD pairs dataset. Trains cross-modal retrieval networks using CNNs for sketch encoding and 3D model encoding, with metric learning for cross-modal alignment.

title: Geometric Deep Learning with A-to-Z BRep Annotations for AI-Assisted CAD Modeling and Reverse Engineering
abstract: Proposes comprehensive A-to-Z annotations for B-rep models covering all geometric and topological elements. Creates large-scale annotated dataset for training geometric deep learning models for CAD modeling assistance and reverse engineering.
method_summary: Defines comprehensive annotation schema covering all B-rep elements (faces, edges, vertices, loops). Generates synthetic training data with mesh perturbations and trains segmentation/classification models on the annotated B-rep data.

title: CNC-Net: Self-Supervised Learning for CNC Machining Operations
abstract: CNC-Net is a self-supervised framework that takes a 3D model as input and generates CNC machine operation parameters. It learns to simulate CNC machining operations from raw materials without manual supervision.
method_summary: Self-supervised framework that learns CNC machining operations by predicting tool paths and parameters from 3D model geometry. Uses differentiable rendering of machining simulation for self-supervised training.

title: Scan-to-BRep: BRep Boundary and Junction Detection for CAD Reverse Engineering
abstract: Proposes a neural network for detecting B-rep boundaries and junctions from 3D scans for CAD reverse engineering. Uses focal loss and non-maximal suppression during training for accurate boundary detection.
method_summary: Learns to detect B-rep structural elements (boundaries between faces, junction points) from point cloud scans. Uses focal loss for class imbalance and NMS for precise localization.

key_results.boundary_detection: accurate B-rep boundary detection from scans

title: Automatic Feature Recognition and Dimensional Attributes Extraction From CAD Models for Hybrid Additive-Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: Proposes Hierarchical Graph Convolutional Neural Network (HGCNN) for recognizing composite additive-subtractive features in CAD models. Extends traditional machining feature recognition to hybrid manufacturing.
method_summary: Hierarchical GCN that processes B-rep graphs at multiple scales to recognize both additive and subtractive manufacturing features. Extracts dimensional attributes for process planning.

title: CSTNet: Constraint-Aware Feature Learning for Parametric Point Cloud
abstract: CSTNet addresses parametric point clouds from CAD shapes by learning both geometric features and constraints inherent in CAD models. Most existing methods only focus on geometric features while overlooking constraints.
method_summary: Dual-branch architecture that jointly learns geometric features from point coordinates and constraint features from CAD parametric relationships. Fuses both branches for constraint-aware shape understanding.

title: Error Notebook-Guided, Training-Free Part Retrieval in 3D CAD Assemblies via Vision-Language Models
abstract: Training-free part retrieval within complex CAD assemblies using vision-language models. Uses error notebook guidance for specification-aware retrieval essential for automated design verification.
method_summary: Leverages pre-trained VLMs with error notebook prompting strategy for zero-shot part retrieval in CAD assemblies. No training required, uses specification-aware matching.

key_results.training_free_retrieval: effective zero-shot part retrieval in assemblies

title: AAGNet: Automatic Machining Feature Recognition using Geometric Attributed Adjacency Graphs
abstract: AAGNet proposes automatic machining feature recognition using a geometric Attributed Adjacency Graph (gAAG) representation that preserves topological, geometric, and extended attributes from neutral B-Rep models. Uses GNN on the gAAG for face-level feature classification.
method_summary: Converts B-rep to geometric Attributed Adjacency Graph preserving topology, geometry, and extended attributes. Applies graph neural network for face-level machining feature classification on the gAAG.

title: BRepGAT: Graph Neural Network to Segment Machining Feature Faces in a B-rep Model
abstract: BRepGAT proposes a graph attention network to segment faces in B-rep models containing machining features. Defines descriptors representing face and edge information from the perspective of feature recognition.
method_summary: Graph attention network operating on B-rep face adjacency graphs. Uses custom face and edge descriptors extracted from B-rep topology for attention-weighted message passing and face segmentation.

key_results.face_segmentation: effective B-rep face segmentation for machining features

title: BrepMFR: Enhancing Machining Feature Recognition in B-rep Models through Deep Learning and Domain Adaptation
abstract: BrepMFR is a deep learning network for machining feature recognition from B-rep models. Transforms B-rep into a unified graph representation and uses domain adaptation to bridge the gap between synthetic and real-world CAD models.
method_summary: Converts B-rep to unified graph with geometric and topological features. Uses domain adaptation techniques to transfer knowledge from synthetic training data to real-world industrial CAD models.

key_results.domain_adaptation: improved generalization to real-world CAD models

title: FeatureNet: Machining Feature Recognition Based on 3D Convolution Neural Network
abstract: FeatureNet learns the distribution of complex machining feature shapes across a large 3D model dataset using 3D CNNs on voxelized representations. Discovers distinguishing features that help in the recognition process automatically.
method_summary: Voxelizes 3D CAD models and applies 3D convolutional neural networks for machining feature classification. Learns shape distributions across feature categories from large synthetic datasets.

key_results.voxel_classification: pioneering deep learning approach for machining feature recognition

title: Geometry based Machining Feature Retrieval with Inductive Transfer Learning
abstract: Approaches machining feature recognition as a retrieval problem using inductive transfer learning. Addresses the challenge that supervised MFR is not feasible for real-time industrial applications due to industry-specific data requirements.
method_summary: Uses transfer learning from pre-trained 3D shape encoders to machining feature retrieval. Inductive approach enables generalization to new feature types without retraining from scratch.

title: Leveraging Vision-Language Models for Manufacturing Feature Recognition in CAD Designs
abstract: Investigates VLMs for automating recognition of manufacturing features in CAD designs without extensive training data. Evaluates GPT-4V and other VLMs on diverse manufacturing feature types, demonstrating zero-shot and few-shot capabilities.
method_summary: Evaluates pre-trained VLMs (GPT-4V, Claude) on manufacturing feature recognition from CAD renderings, comparing zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned approaches against traditional geometric rule-based methods.

title: CADGCL: Unsupervised Retrieval of CAD Models via Boundary Representations
abstract: Unsupervised method for retrieving CAD models based on boundary representations. Transforms B-rep models into graph representations and applies graph contrastive learning for retrieval without labeled data.
method_summary: Converts B-rep to graphs, applies graph contrastive learning with augmentation strategies for unsupervised CAD model retrieval. No labeled training data required.
